title: Introduction to C#
ques: Explain **what C# is** and its relationship with the **.NET Framework**.

answer.content:
*   **What C# is:** C# (pronounced "C-Sharp") is a modern, object-oriented, and type-safe programming language developed by Microsoft.
*   **Relationship with .NET:** C# runs on the .NET Framework (or .NET Core/.NET 5+), which provides a runtime environment (CLR) and a vast class library for building various applications.

explanation: C# is the primary language for .NET development. Code is compiled into Intermediate Language (IL) and executed by the Common Language Runtime (CLR).

title: Hello World
ques: Write a minimal C# program that prints **"Hello, World!"** to the console using the `System` namespace.
explanation: `Main` is the entry point. `Console.WriteLine` prints text followed by a new line. `using System;` allows using `Console` without the `System.` prefix.

title: Variables and Data Types
ques: Declare variables for a **name** (string), **age** (int), and **salary** (double). Assign values and print them.
explanation: C# is statically typed. `string`, `int`, and `double` are aliases for .NET types `String`, `Int32`, and `Double`.String interpolation `$"..."` simplifies formatting.

title: Reading Input
ques: Write a program that asks for the user's **favorite color** and prints a message using that color name.
explanation: `Console.ReadLine()` reads a line of text from the standard input stream (keyboard) as a string.

title: Type Casting
ques: Demonstrate **Implicit** (int to double) and **Explicit** (double to int) casting in C#.
explanation: Implicit casting is automatic for safe conversions (smaller to larger type). Explicit casting requires syntax `(type)value` and may lose data (e.g., truncating decimals).

title: If-Else Statement
ques: Write a C# program to check if a number entered by the user is **Positive**, **Negative**, or **Zero**.
explanation: `int.Parse()` converts the string input to an integer. The `if-else if-else` structure handles multiple conditions.

title: Switch Statement
ques: Use a `switch` statement to print the name of the day based on an integer input (1 for Monday, etc.).
explanation: The `switch` statement selects a code block to execute based on a match with the `case` value. `break` exits the switch block.

title: Foreach Loop
ques: Create an array of strings containing **fruit names**. Use a `foreach` loop to print each fruit.
explanation: `foreach` is designed to iterate over collections (arrays, lists). It is cleaner and safer than a `for` loop when index access isn't needed.

title: Methods
ques: Define a static method `Greetings` that takes a **name** as a parameter and prints "Welcome, [name]!". Call it from Main.
explanation: Methods are blocks of code that perform a specific task. `static` means it belongs to the class and can be called without creating an instance of `Program`.

title: Method Overloading
ques: Overload a method `Add`. One version adds two **integers**, and another adds two **doubles**.
explanation: Method overloading allows multiple methods with the same name but different parameter lists (types or count).
